core.title: security
core.description: financial instrument that can be bought or sold

core.title: debt instrument
core.description: financial instrument and credit agreement evidencing monies owed by the issuer to the holder on terms as specified

core.title: tradable debt instrument
core.description: a debt instrument that is also a security, i.e., that can be bought and sold by the holder

core.title: equity instrument
core.description: financial instrument representing an ownership interest in an entity or pool of assets

core.title: share
core.description: financial instrument that signifies a unit of equity ownership in a corporation, or a unit of ownership in a mutual fund, or interest in a general or limited partnership, or a unit of ownership in a structured product, such as a real estate investment trust

core.title: listed security
core.description: registered security listed on at least one exchange

core.title: security form
core.description: nature of the proof of ownership of a security

core.title: bond
core.description: tradable debt instrument representing a loan in which the issuer owes the holder(s) a debt

core.title: government bond
core.description: debt security issued by a government to fund government spending

core.title: corporate bond
core.description: bond issued by a company in order to raise financing

core.title: municipal bond
core.description: government bond that may be issued by a regional, rather than national, authority

core.title: zero coupon bond
core.description: bond issued with a coupon rate of zero and that trades at a deep discount to face value

core.title: fixed coupon bond
core.description: bond whose coupon rate and principal amount are fixed at the time of origination or sale and remain constant while the security is outstanding

core.title: convertible bond
core.description: bond that gives the holder the right to convert the bond into a fixed number of shares (conversion ratio) if the equity price rises above a specified level (strike price)

core.title: callable bond
core.description: bond that includes a stipulation allowing the issuer the right to repurchase and retire the bond at the call price after the call protection period

core.title: floating rate note
core.description: bond with a variable interest rate based on a published reference interest rate

core.title: commercial paper
core.description: an unsecured short-term debt instrument typically issued by a bank, corporation, or foreign government to obtain funds to meet short-term debt obligations, such as accounts receivable, inventories, or payroll, backed only by an issuing bank or company promise to pay the face amount on the maturity date specified on the note

core.title: treasury bill
core.description: short-term zero coupon treasury obligation with a maturity ranging from one to twelve months

core.title: bond coupon
core.description: interest rate on a debt security that the issuer promises to pay to the holder until maturity, expressed as an annual percentage of the face value

core.title: sovereign debt instrument
core.description: debt security issued by the government of a country

core.title: common share
core.description: share that signifies a unit of ownership in a corporation and represents a claim on part of the corporation's assets and earnings

core.title: preferred share
core.description: share that pays dividends at a specified rate and has preference over common shares in the payment of dividends and liquidation of corporate assets

core.title: non-voting share
core.description: share that has no voting right

core.title: partially paid share
core.description: share whose payment status indicates that only a portion of the market value has been received by the company for the shares

core.title: depositary receipt
core.description: negotiable financial instrument issued by a trust company, security depositary, or bank that is evidence of the deposit of publicly traded securities and that facilitates the ownership of securities traded in other jurisdictions

core.title: American depositary receipt
core.description: depositary receipt that represents securities of a foreign company and allows that company's securities to trade in the U.S. financial markets

core.title: global depositary receipt
core.description: depositary receipt where a certificate issued by a depositary bank, which purchases securities of foreign companies, creates a security on a local exchange backed by those securities

core.title: dividend
core.description: announced commitment to make a specific distribution of a portion of earnings to shareholders, prorated by class of security

core.title: fixed rate dividend
core.description: dividend that provides a specified annual return on the nominal value (and any premium) paid on shares

core.title: share yield
core.description: ratio of the annualized dividend per share divided by the (current) price per share

core.title: asset-backed security
core.description: debt instrument backed by receivables other than those arising out of real estate loans or mortgages

core.title: mortgage-backed security
core.description: debt obligations that represent claims to the cash flows from pools of mortgage loans, most commonly on residential property

core.title: collateralized debt obligation
core.description: structured finance constructed from a portfolio of fixed income assets including corporate loans and mortgage backed securities. A special purpose vehicle (SPV) issues notes to investors in order to raise funds that are invested in a portfolio of those fixed income assets, held by the SPV as collateral for the notes. Further notes: Collateralized Debt Obligation, for example, ABS CDO which consists of a portfolio of different ABS bonds, and the payments to the holders of these trust certificates are derived from the cash flows of the ABS bonds. This CDO instrument is part of a CDO issue, consisting of individual CDO instruments of a given seniority. Often referred to as tranches and slices (Investopedia). Investopedia: Similar in structure to a collateralized mortgage obligation (CMO) or collateralized bond obligation (CBO), CDOs are unique in that they represent different types of debt and credit risk. In the case of CDOs, these different types of debt are often referred to as 'tranches' or 'slices'. Each slice has a different maturity and risk associated with it. The higher the risk, the more the CDO pays. Further details: Collateralized Debt obligations are securitized interests in pools of - generally non-mortgage - assets. Assets - called collateral - usually comprise loans or debt instruments. A CDO may be called a collateralized loan obligation (CLO) or collateralized bond obligation (CBO) if it holds only loans or bonds, respectively. Investors bear the credit risk of the collateral. Multiple tranches of securities are issued by the CDO, offering investors various maturity and credit risk characteristics.

core.title: tranche
core.description: segment of a pool of securities, typically debt instruments

core.title: pool
core.description: combination of resources for a common purpose or benefit

core.title: m b s pool
core.description: A pool investment consisting of a collection of MBS instruments.

core.title: c d o pool
core.description: A pool of CDO securities. The underlying of the CDO Squared is a pool of CDO instruments.

core.title: collective investment vehicle
core.description: assets pooled by investors whose share capital remains separate from the assets of the vehicle

core.title: mutual fund
core.description: open-end professionally managed investment fund established for the purpose of investing in securities such as stocks, bonds, money market instruments and similar assets

core.title: exchange-traded fund
core.description: investment fund whose fund units are traded on an exchange, much like stocks

core.title: hedge fund
core.description: investment fund that pursues a total return and is usually open to qualified investors only

core.title: equity fund
core.description: A fund which invests in at least 85% shares.

core.title: fund unit
core.description: quantified share of beneficial interest in a pooled fund, representing a proportional claim on the fund's assets, income, or entitlements

core.title: issuer
core.description: role of a party that issues (or proposes to issue in a formal filing) one or more financial instruments

core.title: share issuer
core.description: issuer of securities that represent an ownership interest in something

core.title: m b s issuer
core.description: The issuer of a Mortgage Backed Security.

core.title: security underwriter
core.description: party that has purchased from an issuer with a view to, or sells for an issuer in connection with, the distribution of any security, or participates or has a direct or indirect participation in any such undertaking, or participates or has a participation in the direct or indirect underwriting of any such undertaking

core.title: underwriter
core.description: financial service provider that evaluates and assumes another party's risk for a fee, such as a commission, premium, spread or interest

core.title: security underwriting arrangement
core.description: underwriting agreement between an organization (typically an investment bank) and a securities issuer that commits the underwriter to assuming risk involved in buying a new issue of securities and reselling it to the public

core.title: prospectus
core.description: formal, written offering document to sell securities that provides the facts an investor needs to make an informed investment decision

core.title: international securities identification number
core.description: security identifier that is defined as specified in ISO 6166, Securities and related financial instruments -- International securities identification numbering system (ISIN)

core.title: security registry
core.description: registry used to manage security identifiers and related information

core.title: listing
core.description: catalog entry for a securities offering managed by an exchange that provides the terms under which that security is made available on that exchange

core.title: basket of securities
core.description: basket composed of securities, typically of a particular asset class such as equities or bonds

core.title: portfolio
core.description: collection of investments (financial assets) such as stocks, bonds and cash equivalents, as well as mutual funds, real estate, and so forth

core.title: weighted average maturity
core.description: weighted average amount of time until the maturities on mortgages in a mortgage-backed security (MBS)

core.title: redemption schedule
core.description: repayment schedule whereby a given percentage of a bond issue is redeemed on predefined dates

core.title: redemption event
core.description: a prescriptive event involving the return of an investor's principal in a fixed-income security, such as a preferred stock or bond, or the sale of units in a mutual fund

core.title: yield
core.description: return on the investor's capital investment
